nodes.label: PRD — Iron Throne of Ashes (v1 archive)
Doom Toll — 13-space shared loss condition track
nodes.rationale: Creates a shared stakes floor that forces negotiation every round. The leading player has the most to lose if it advances; trailing players can exploit that. Advance/recede triggers create a pressure system without player elimination.

nodes.label: Broken Court State — no-elimination replacement mechanic
nodes.rationale: Replaces player elimination entirely. Reduced to 1 action/turn but retains full Voting Phase participation. Primary accessibility signal for the 35-55 demographic. Enables rescue as a social mechanic.

nodes.label: Blood Pact Mode — traitor variant
nodes.rationale: Ships at launch as an acquisition mechanic. App delivers the traitor card privately (no shuffle tell). Three focus group personas named it a purchase driver before playing. Traitor wins only if Doom Toll reaches 13.

nodes.label: Rescue Mechanic — active Arch-Regent rescues Broken Court player
nodes.rationale: The emotional peak of the average session per focus group. 2.8 activations/game observed in v1 simulation — highest of any Alliance Engine variant. Five of eight personas named it most anticipated moment. Creates the sentence 'I rescued my rival because I needed his vote.'

nodes.label: Shadowking Behavior System — autonomous AI antagonist driven by Behavior Deck
nodes.rationale: Not a player — a system. Deterministic from seed. 20-card Behavior Deck drives threat each round. Target SK win rate 18-22% calibrated through simulation. Forces players to cooperate or face shared loss.

nodes.label: Voting Phase — COUNTER/ABSTAIN unanimous vote to block Behavior Cards
nodes.rationale: Political engine of the game. Cooperation is expensive (spends Fate Cards); abstention is dangerous (advances Doom Toll). Creates 'check the leader' dynamic — trailing players can tax the frontrunner by abstaining.

nodes.label: Herald Diplomatic Action — solo Herald reduces Doom Toll at Dark Fortress
nodes.rationale: Non-combat Doom reduction path. Prevents combat-only doom reduction loop. Raj's actionable from focus group post-playtest. Narratively coherent: a Herald walking alone into the enemy fortress to negotiate.

nodes.label: War Field — simultaneous Fate Card combat resolution system
Behavior Deck — 20-card Shadowking action deck (6 SPAWN/6 MOVE/4 CLAIM/3 ASSAULT/1 ESCALATE)
nodes.rationale: Post-fix composition: ESCALATE reduced 2→1, MOVE increased 5→6, to target Dark Lord win rate 18-22%.

nodes.label: Tutorial: Into the Blighted Wastes — 5-turn mandatory tutorial
War Banners — single unified resource for all player actions

edges.source_location: F-006 — non-unanimous vote advances Doom Toll
F-011b — Blood Pact wins when Doom Toll reaches 13
Both describe Alliance Engine v1.0 Iron Throne variant

hyperedges.label: Doom Pressure System — Voting Phase + Doom Toll + Behavior Deck together create cooperative pressure
hyperedges.description: These four concepts operate as a single integrated pressure system: the Behavior Deck drives the Shadowking each round, the Voting Phase gives players a costly chance to block it, and failures advance the Doom Toll toward the shared loss condition. No element works without the others.
